summary: Full backend + frontend testing of new Discord-style avatar Decorations feature. Backend pytest 11/11 passed (1 skipped — happy-path equip requires admin to own a decoration; admin has 52 gems vs 3000 price). Frontend verified end-to-end via Playwright on the live preview URL: Shop has all 6 tabs (Boosts/Colors/Icons/Anims/Banners/Decors), Decors tab renders exactly 50 .deco-wrap cards each priced 3,000 gems, MyProfilePage has the new Decorations accordion (data-testid=equip-decos) plus the existing 4 sections, the profile-preview avatar is wrapped in .deco-wrap, and the public profile /profile/Admin6419 also wraps the avatar in .deco-wrap. All previously-validated flows (login both modes, existing shop tabs, equip Title/Icon/Anim/Banner) continue to work.

issues: All 50 decoration preview avatars render as the same flat dark-blue circle because no .deco-* CSS class is applied to the preview <div className=`deco-wrap`> in DecorationsGrid (ShopPage.js:322). The animated ::before/::after rings only appear on the actual avatars (profile/public), not on the shop preview cards. Consider passing item.css to the preview wrapper so users can see the animation before buying.
Carry-over from iter_7/8/9: 'Level N -- Name' double-dash separator on level badge — purely cosmetic.

passed_tests: Backend pytest 11/11 PASSED, 1 SKIPPED (happy-path equip — admin owns no decoration)
decorations array has exactly 50 items, all priced 3000 gems, all css starts with 'deco-'
Known keys present: deco_flame_ring, deco_frost_ring, deco_supernova_burst, deco_galaxy_spiral
POST /api/shop/buy-profile-item with insufficient gems → 400 'not enough gems'
Both admin@habitrpg.com (Game Mode) and test@habitrpg.com (Focus Mode) login OK
Frontend Shop page has all 6 tabs visible: Boosts, Colors, Icons, Anims, Banners, Decors
Decors tab shows 50 .deco-wrap cards (count via DOM query)
Decors tab shows '3,000' label 100 times (50 items × subtitle + buy button)
MyProfilePage has equip-decos accordion alongside equip-titles/icons/anims/banners
MyProfilePage profile preview avatar wrapped in .deco-wrap div
Decorations accordion empty-state copy: 'Buy avatar decorations from the Shop (3,000 gems each) for animated frames around your avatar.'
Public profile /profile/Admin6419 has 1 .deco-wrap on the avatar
action_items: (Minor UX) In ShopPage.js DecorationsGrid (line ~322), apply item.css to the preview <div className=`deco-wrap`> so the shop preview shows the actual animated ring, e.g. `<div className={`deco-wrap ${item.css}`}>`. Currently all 50 cards look identical (plain dark-blue circle).
(Cosmetic, carry-over from iter_7/8/9) Fix 'Level N -- Name' double-dash separator on public profile level badge.
(Optional) Seed admin (or a TEST_ user) with one decoration to allow happy-path equip-and-persist regression to run automatically without skip.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py SHOP_DECORATIONS dict has all 50 entries with consistent shape (name, css, price=3000); equip and buy endpoints correctly include 'decoration' branch and use 'unlocked_decorations' / 'equipped_decoration' fields.
buy-profile-item endpoint comment still says 'Buy a profile item (icon, animation, banner)' — should be updated to mention decoration.
/api/profile/me/equip correctly accepts None to remove decoration (verified via curl + pytest).

ShopPage.js DecorationsGrid does NOT pass item.css to the preview wrapper — only the literal class 'deco-wrap' is applied, so preview ring animations are missing in the shop. Other grids (Icons, Banners) have the same render concept but those use SVG/inline styles; decorations rely on CSS pseudo-elements and need the per-item class.

MyProfilePage.js correctly wraps the live-preview avatar with `${decoClass}` (line 105) and the per-pill mini-preview also uses `${d.css}` (line 320) — so the equip section will display correctly once admin owns a decoration.
PublicProfilePage.js correctly applies decoClass to the public avatar wrapper (line 91).

index.css contains 50 distinct .deco-* classes plus shared .deco-wrap base + .deco-wrap::before/::after rules — count verified via grep (62 matches total = 50 main + 1 base + .deco-wrap::before/::after + a few shared keyframes).

success_rate.backend: 100% (11 passed, 1 skipped due to insufficient gems on admin)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on requested features; 1 minor UX nit on shop preview rendering

seed_data_creation: None. Admin equipped_decoration was briefly set to null and verified persisted; left as null (matches pre-test state since admin owns no decoration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Decorations feature is fully wired end-to-end. Backend: SHOP_DECORATIONS=50 items @3000 gems in server.py:381; user fields equipped_decoration / unlocked_decorations added; buy/equip/profile-items/public-profile endpoints all support type='decoration'. Frontend: ShopPage Decors tab + MyProfilePage equip-decos accordion + .deco-wrap on both avatars (My + Public). 50 .deco-* CSS classes in index.css. Admin (Admin6419) has 52 gems and owns no decorations, so to test buy/equip happy paths, either grant gems via DB or seed a TEST_ user with unlocked_decorations:['deco_flame_ring']. Known minor UX issue: ShopPage DecorationsGrid preview wrapper missing item.css class — see action_items.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. The only finding is a UX gap where shop preview cards don't show the animated ring (because the per-item CSS class isn't applied to the preview deco-wrap div in ShopPage.js:322). Fix is a 1-line interpolation change.
